titleStmt.title: Letter from K. Casey to Willie Daly, October 1923
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: K. Casey

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
I got
the address
when I had
this letter written P41/1/15 Roxboro
Oct 24th My Dear Willie, It is about
time that I should think
of you in fact I am a long
time thinking of writing to you
I am sure you felt bad about
poor Charlie he is in heaven
I hope I am sure that it is all the
good people that is done to
death our Lord had to suffer
for our sins & he forgave his
enemies I did not see your mother
or father since the election I am
sure you felt delighted to have
cousin John get elected
our pricts pulled well for
him all the same he did
not get too many votes in
this parish do you hear
often from Tom & is he getting
on good. I am sure he is not
in one camp with you. It was
a very bad summer.questionable reading
is asking for the turf every
sunday I suppose he is getting
very cold. You heard questionable reading
got married to May at
Patrick O'Sullivans. Dan
took a trip away since
you left he is much improved
I hope you will be coming
home when this letter will
reach you as a many are
leaving out & some are
arresting as well Cronin
at Brachil was taken last
sunday. Nealy Curtin was
left home I am sending
you some cigarettes I hope
you will get them alright I had
not your address so I put
on Peter Costeloes name
Hoping you are good.
From K. Casey
Roxboro
I am sending you your double
packages of fags I hope
the government will soon
leave ye out as your is
preaching to girls get
married at 14 years &
boys at 17 so he ought
speak for our prisoners
to release them & marry
them he would make
a little penny as the
times is very bad with
him no pigs or cattle
buying only for a little
butter.
Goodbye from Conn
K. Casey
